note: Seed of the public validation suite. Every case is synthetic with a fully published derivation, so inputs and expected values can be redistributed freely. NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook datasets (public domain) are the next wave; AIAG MSA examples will be verified privately and never shipped (copyright).

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 6.4
cases.derivation: x=[10,12,11,13,12]: mean=11.6; MRs=[2,1,2,1], MRbar=1.5; sigma=MRbar/d2(2) with d2(2)=2/sqrt(pi), so sigma=1.5*sqrt(pi)/2; I-limits=11.6 +/- 3*sigma; MR UCL=D4(2)*MRbar with D4(2)=1+3*d3(2)/d2(2), d3(2)=sqrt(2-4/pi).

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 6.2
cases.derivation: Subgroups [[10,12],[11,13],[9,11],[12,14],[10,12]] (n=2, k=5): means=[11,12,10,13,11], xbarbar=11.4; ranges all 2, Rbar=2; sigma=Rbar/d2(2)=sqrt(pi); xbar limits=11.4 +/- 3*sigma/sqrt(2); R UCL=D4(2)*Rbar.

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 6.3
cases.derivation: Same subgroups: s of each pair {a,a+2} = sqrt(2), sbar=sqrt(2); sigma=sbar/c4(2) with c4(2)=sqrt(2/pi), so sigma=sqrt(pi); xbar limits as in xbar_r; S UCL=B4(2)*sbar with B4(2)=1+3*sqrt(1-c4^2)/c4.

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 7.2
cases.derivation: defectives=[2,3,1,4,2], n=50 each: pbar=12/250=0.048; sigma=sqrt(pbar*(1-pbar)/50); UCL=pbar+3*sigma; LCL=max(0, pbar-3*sigma)=0 (clamped).

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 7.2.4
cases.derivation: Same defectives, n=50: center=n*pbar=2.4; UCL=2.4+3*sqrt(2.4*(1-0.048)); LCL clamped to 0.

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 7.3.1
Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 7.3.2
cases.derivation: defects=[6,4,9,5] over units=[3,2,4,2]: ubar=24/11; per-point limits ubar +/- 3*sqrt(ubar/n_i), so no scalar UCL/LCL in stats (varying sizes).

cases.source: Hand derivation; indices and intervals per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 8 (Cp interval exact chi-square; Cpk interval Bissell 1990 / Montgomery eq. 8.19)
cases.derivation: x=[10,12,11,13,12], LSL=6, USL=16, confidence 0.95: mean=11.6; sigma_within=MRbar/d2(2)=1.5*sqrt(pi)/2; sigma_overall=sqrt(5.2/4); Cp=10/(6*s_w); Cpk=min(4.4,5.6)/(3*s_w); Pp,Ppk analog with s_o. df_within=0.62*(n-1)=2.48 (MR(2) effective df), df_overall=4. Cp CI: Cp*sqrt(chi2(alpha/2,df)/df); Cpk CI: Cpk*(1 +/- z*sqrt(1/(9*n*Cpk^2)+1/(2*df))). Expected PPM from normal tails.

cases.source: Hand derivation; runs-about-median test with normal approximation

NIST StRD Univariate Summary Statistics, dataset Michelso (public domain): certified mean 299.852400000000, certified std 0.0790105478190518; https://www.itl.nist.gov/div898/strd/univ/data/Michelso.dat
cases.derivation: mean and sigma_overall must reproduce the NIST-certified values; pp derived from the certified std with specs LSL=299.6, USL=300.1: pp = 0.5/(6*0.0790105478190518).

cases.source: NIST StRD dataset NumAcc1 (numerical accuracy benchmark, public domain): certified mean 10000002 (exact), certified std 1 (exact); https://www.itl.nist.gov/div898/strd/univ/data/NumAcc1.dat
cases.derivation: Large-offset data probes catastrophic cancellation in the variance computation. With LSL=9999999, USL=10000005: pp = 6/(6*1) = 1 exactly.

cases.source: NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook of Statistical Methods, section 6.3.2.4 (EWMA Control Charts), public domain; https://www.itl.nist.gov/div898/handbook/pmc/section3/pmc324.htm
cases.derivation: Handbook worked example: center 50, historical s 2.0539, lambda 0.3, k=3, asymptotic limits. Handbook prints UCL=52.5884 and LCL=47.4115 (the LCL is NIST's own rounding of 47.41157); tolerance 1e-3 covers the 4-decimal display precision.

cases.source: Hand derivation; tabular CUSUM per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 9.1
cases.derivation: x=[10,10,10,11,12,13], center=10, sigma=1, k=0.5, h=4: K=0.5, H=4. C+ accumulates max(0, x-(10.5)+prev): [0,0,0,0.5,2.0,4.5]; C- stays 0. The last point exceeds H=4 (verified in the unit tests via the table); stats checked here: decision interval H = h*sigma = 4.
